policy: Executable route-domain replay may run only when Experiments imports real Core and ProtoKits through stable package, workspace, or path wiring. The Signal Bastion strategic-pressure gate is now satisfied through package wiring and an executable smoke; future lanes must still avoid duplicating reusable kit simulation in Experiments.

gates.reason: Signal Bastion is the strongest ProtoKit-backed replay lane. Experiments now declares local package wiring for Core `nexusrealtime` and ProtoKits `@luminarylabs/nexusrealtime-protokits`, and the executable smoke imports the real generic-defense DSK aliases instead of copying or re-implementing defense simulation under Experiments.

requiredLocalPackageWiring.requiredFor: createRealtimeGame and fixed-tick runtime import in Node smoke tests
generic-defense DSK alias import without the route owning simulation

satisfiedBy: tests/signal-bastion-executable-route-replay-smoke.mjs imports real Core plus `@luminarylabs/nexusrealtime-protokits/generic-defense-dsk-boundaries` and advances the checked fixed-tick plan.

executableReplayCoverage.coverage: real Core plus ProtoKits generic-defense DSK aliases, Signal Bastion debug preset, fixed ticks, semantic route inputs, deterministic resource/snapshot/descriptor digest, and browser ownership exclusions

forbiddenFallbacks: browser CDN imports inside Node replay smoke
DOM or Canvas shim that owns reusable simulation
new V1/V2/V3 Signal Bastion route fork just to satisfy replay
nextMainBranchPatchPlan: Keep the executable replay in the full check suite while dependency install behavior settles; do not move it into deploy checks until package install latency and stability are known.

Migrate the browser Signal Bastion boot from the broad generic-defense compatibility facade toward the smallest proven generic-defense DSK aliases, while leaving Canvas/HUD/audio/assets in the route host.
Use the same package-wired executable replay pattern for the next route lane only when a real ProtoKit boundary exists; otherwise keep the lane contract-only and avoid route-local simulation copies.
gates.pruningEffect: This gate now permits a real Signal Bastion route-domain replay while continuing to block fake local replays, copied ProtoKit fixtures, browser-owned simulation shims, and route forks. Route pruning should still wait for browser host migration evidence before deleting compatibility paths.
